title: --force flag + options param for initFabric — overwrite all 5 layers
description: Extend initFabric() to accept options: { force?, skipBootstrap?, skipMcp?, skipHooks? } (all optional for backward compatibility). Add --force, --no-bootstrap, --no-mcp, --no-hooks flags to the citty args schema. When force=true: bypass all 3 ABORT guards, overwrite in writeNewFile(), overwrite in copyTemplateIfMissing(), and force re-merge in mergeClaudeStopHook(). Add i18n keys in cli.init.force.* namespace. Create packages/cli/__tests__/init-force.test.ts with 5 test cases.

files.change: Refactor initFabric(target, options?) signature; wrap 3 existsSync ABORT guards with && !options?.force; update writeNewFile to overwrite when force; update copyTemplateIfMissing to overwrite when force; update mergeClaudeStopHook to replace hook entry when force; add force/no-bootstrap/no-mcp/no-hooks to citty args schema and InitArgs type.

Add cli.init.args.force.description, cli.init.args.no-bootstrap.description, cli.init.args.no-mcp.description, cli.init.args.no-hooks.description, cli.init.force.overwritten (label), cli.init.force.warning keys; also migrate the raw ABORT template literals in init.ts to use the existing cli.init.errors.abort-existing key.
Mirror all new keys added to en.ts with Chinese translations.

New test file: 5 test cases covering (a) --force overwrites existing AGENTS.md, (b) overwrites .claude/skills/*, (c) overwrites .claude/hooks/*, (d) overwrites/re-merges .claude/settings.json Stop hook, (e) --no-bootstrap/--no-mcp/--no-hooks each skip respective stage; plus regression case that plain init without --force still aborts.

implementation: Update InitArgs type alias near L20-23 to add: force?: boolean; skipBootstrap?: boolean; skipMcp?: boolean; skipHooks?: boolean.
Add all four flags to the citty args block at L64-73 following the check-only pattern from sync-meta.ts:L35-39 (type: 'boolean', default: false).
Change initFabric() signature from initFabric(target: string) to initFabric(target: string, options?: { force?: boolean; skipBootstrap?: boolean; skipMcp?: boolean; skipHooks?: boolean }).
Wrap the three existsSync ABORT guards at L140-149: add && !options?.force to each condition; when force is true and path exists, call rmSync() before the write functions rather than throwing.
Update writeNewFile() helper at L273-279: if options?.force is true, skip the existsSync guard and call writeFileSync unconditionally.
Update copyTemplateIfMissing() at L281-289: if options?.force is true, remove the early-return on exists and always call copyFileSync.
Update mergeClaudeStopHook() at L301-359: when options?.force is true, replace the existing hook entry in settings.json instead of returning 'skipped'.
Add CLI argument wiring in initCommand.run() to pass force/skipBootstrap/skipMcp/skipHooks from args into the options object passed to initFabric.
Add all new i18n keys to en.ts and zh-CN.ts; migrate the three raw ABORT template literals to use t('cli.init.errors.abort-existing', { path }).

Create packages/cli/__tests__/init-force.test.ts following the pattern from init-nondestructive.test.ts: import initFabric, use createWerewolfFixtureRoot + cleanupFixtureRoot, write pre-existing files with writeFixtureFile, call initFabric(target, { force: true }), assert overwritten content.

reference.pattern: initFabric() exported function receives target + optional options; citty args wired in command handler then passed as options object; all 5 write sites checked for force bypass
reference.examples: exploration-patterns.json (force_injection_pattern): wrap guard with && !args.force; test pattern: createWerewolfFixtureRoot + writeFixtureFile + expect not.toThrow

criteria: 'fabric init' on a fresh project: all files created, no errors (unchanged behavior)
'fabric init' on an already-initialized project without --force: throws ABORT error (unchanged default)
'fabric init --force' on an already-initialized project: overwrites all 5 artifact layers and prints warning message
All existing tests in init-nondestructive.test.ts pass without modification
All 5 new test cases in init-force.test.ts pass
Both en.ts and zh-CN.ts updated with identical key sets for all new cli.init.force.* keys

rationale.chosen_approach: Extend initFabric() with optional options param — backward-compatible since options defaults to undefined
alternatives_considered: Delete blocking files in command handler before calling initFabric() — would keep initFabric() signature stable but hide force semantics from the exported API
Separate 'fabric init --overwrite' subcommand — more visible but adds surface area and complicates testing
decision_factors: User explicitly specified initFabric() signature: options: { force?, skipBootstrap?, skipMcp?, skipHooks? }
Optional param is backward-compatible — external callers that pass only target string continue to work
Keeping force logic inside initFabric() makes test coverage direct and complete
rationale.tradeoffs: Slightly larger function signature, but cleaner than pre-deleting files in the handler

manual_checks: fabric init on clean fixture: creates all 7 artifacts
fabric init on pre-initialized fixture: prints ABORT and exits non-zero
fabric init --force on pre-initialized fixture: all artifacts overwritten, warning printed, exits 0
success_metrics: init-force.test.ts: all 5 new test cases pass
unit: init-force: re-merges claude settings stop hook
